metric_value: Feature-rich and aggressively priced; most capable offering vs. Cisco and ALANTEC
notes: Aberdeen states 2500 is 'unlike other Ethernet/FDDI switches available from Cisco Systems and ALANTEC' in feature richness and pricing.

metric_value: 565,000 pps (packets per second)
notes: Achieved via ISE-chip ASIC + dual RISC processors. Aberdeen states this puts LANplex 2500 'well ahead of competitive offerings from Cisco, Bay and ALANTEC'.

metric_value: 6 to 9 months ahead on performance and features
notes: Aberdeen notes competition is intensifying, cutting into 3Com's lead.

metric_value: Backbone switching market leader
notes: Even new 2500 customers gained credibility via 3Com's established LANplex 6000 market leadership.

metric_value: ASIC front-end 'fast path' + dual RISC processors; repetitive packet processing in ASIC, intelligent functions in RISC
notes: Design reduces component count, improving reliability. No central processing engine ensures performance scales with new features.

metric_value: Introduced first in LANplex 2500; next to be added to LANplex 6000
notes: ISE-chip ASIC is new to the LANplex family starting with the 2500.

metric_value: Collapsed backbone with server farm consolidation in data center; complex functions centralized in router
notes: Stage 1 of 3Com's three-stage High-Performance Scalable Networking vision.

metric_value: Distributed LAN switches in wiring closets; FDDI and Fast Ethernet high-speed links to servers and desktops
notes: LANplex 2500 is the Stage 2 deliverable. High-speed uplinks include FDDI and Fast Ethernet.

metric_value: ATM migration beginning with campus backbone, extended to buildings where cost/performance justifies
notes: Stage 3 is the planned ATM migration endpoint of the HPSN vision.

metric_name: OC-3 ATM uplink card for LANplex 2500 — ship date prediction
notes: OC-3 uplink with LAN emulation and UNI connectivity software projected for Q1 1996.

metric_value: Users plan ATM backbone implementation in 1996 timeframe
notes: Based on interviews with 2500 users; number of users noted 1996 ATM backbone plans.

metric_name: Fast Ethernet (100BaseTX) uplink card for LANplex 2500 — ship date prediction
metric_value: Early 1996 (Q1)
notes: Users noted strong interest in Fast Ethernet card expected to ship by early 1996.

metric_name: FDDI campus backbone status in enterprise planning
metric_value: Still prominent as active deliverable on planning agendas of many large enterprises
notes: Despite ATM excitement and increased shipments, FDDI remains the dominant campus backbone technology.

metric_value: LANplex 2500 'came through in these environments with flying colors'
notes: Product stability is a big issue for users needing to quickly drop switch into production environments. Aberdeen user quotes: 'there is no convenient time to take our network down.'

metric_value: Device must be fully flash PROM and fully downloadable from central site; no forklift upgrades acceptable
notes: LANplex 2500 meets this requirement per Aberdeen user interviews.

metric_value: Ethernet, FDDI, 565K pps (3Com) vs. IP/IPX routing with AT in Q3'95 (Cisco) vs. superior but not as complete as Cisco (Bay/ALANTEC)
notes: Source: Aberdeen Group competitive table, September 1995.

metric_value: 3Com: IP routing available; Cisco: IP/IPX routing with AT in Q3'95; Bay: will have key routing protocols like ALANTEC
notes: Routing capabilities comparison across major competitors.

metric_value: 3Com: FDDI today + Fast Ethernet and ATM; Cisco: ambitious timeframe; Bay/ALANTEC: all players have indicated support
notes: Uplink roadmap comparison for Fast Ethernet and ATM uplink support.

metric_value: 3Com: redundant power and hot-swap modules; Cisco and 3Com appear stronger on reliability
notes: Redundant power supply and hot-swap modules cited as reliability advantage.

metric_value: Acquired 1993; formed core of 3Com Switching Division
notes: Synernetics' expertise in high-performance FDDI backbone switching formed the foundation of 3Com's LANplex family.

metric_value: Up to 16 switched 10 Mbps Ethernet ports and 2 switched FDDI ports
notes: Modular, intelligent workgroup switch. Growth path to Fast Ethernet and ATM.

metric_value: Breaks FDDI packets (max 4500 bytes) into Ethernet frames (max 1500 bytes) for FDDI-to-Ethernet translation
notes: Critical for high-performance server farms driving wire-speed links across FDDI/Ethernet boundary.

metric_value: Guaranteed minimum buffers per port with dynamic expansion; reduces packet drops under port contention
notes: User-defined packet filtering also enables traffic control based on thresholds and specific characteristics.

metric_value: Any port can be designated a roving RMON port; unlimited roving ports per switch; works across multiple switches
notes: Distinguished from typical single-port RMON. 3Com also supports standard RMON from external hardware/software sources.

metric_value: RFC 1271 support enabling RMON access across all ports; developing in-box RMON agent
notes: Aberdeen states this capability is 'important' and 3Com has recognized the need for software-based RMON in-box.

metric_name: Transcend Enterprise Manager — Windows version ship date
notes: Flagship management app already supported HP, Sun, and IBM platform solutions.

metric_name: Cisco defensive position on telnet support
metric_value: Telnet server connectivity requirement puts Cisco on the defensive; 3Com lead expected to be short-lived
notes: User demand for telnet-based troubleshooting and software downline loading cited as competitive differentiator for 3Com.

metric_value: Switched Ethernet client connections to FDDI-based servers on campus backbone; replaces expensive router-based FDDI connections
notes: Users deploy 2500 in hospitals, financial institutions, universities, and manufacturing companies.

metric_value: Isolated server traffic on separate backbone using second FDDI port; departmental backbone with local servers
notes: Wiring hub floor devices often connected directly to 2500 via its multiple MAC address capabilities.

metric_value: LANplex technology repackaged in SuperStack form factor for SMB/stackable segment
notes: 16-port Ethernet switch with single FDDI port. Low-cost alternative to LANplex 2500 leveraging same ASIC technology.

metric_value: Virtual Networking cited for security, bandwidth management, and ease of moves/adds/changes
notes: LANplex 2500 supports VLANs via port groups and MAC address groups. IP/IPX/AppleTalk intranetworking routing software included.

metric_value: Technology leadership through proprietary ASIC development; fewer components, higher reliability, better price/performance
notes: 3Com 'took a leadership role in switching by pushing the technology envelope, moving aggressively to ASIC technology.'

metric_value: Large number of early 2500 customers had existing LANplex 6000 installations or were evaluating 6000
notes: Attractive 2500 price/performance pushed 6000-leaning users to install 2500 even as new product in early 1995.

metric_value: LANplex 2500 has met and fully exceeded its promise as excellent workgroup solution and flagship revenue performer for 3Com Switching Division
notes: Aberdeen notes product is 'doing extremely well in the market' and has built its own identity independent of the LANplex 6000.

metric_value: 3Com must deliver Fast Ethernet and ATM uplink promise to maintain leadership; density improvements also expected
notes: Aberdeen frames this as the critical next step: 'deliver the promise of uplink flexibility to assure that users can take advantage of the full gamut of high-speed server connectivity.'

metric_name: Fast Ethernet uplink card — actual release
metric_value: 3Com shipped Fast Ethernet (100BaseTX) uplink cards for LANplex in 1996 as predicted; Fast Ethernet became dominant LAN technology by 1998
notes: Prediction (OBS-031) confirmed: 100BaseTX uplinks shipped. Fast Ethernet overtook FDDI as backbone; 3Com LANplex line discontinued ~2000 after HP acquisition.

metric_name: OC-3 ATM uplink card — actual outcome
metric_value: ATM uplinks shipped but ATM failed to achieve predicted workgroup dominance; Gigabit Ethernet emerged as alternative by 1997-1998
notes: Prediction (OBS-032) partially correct: ATM cards shipped but ATM lost broader market battle to Fast/Gigabit Ethernet by 1998. Ethernet proved more cost-effective.

metric_value: FDDI remained enterprise backbone technology through ~1997 then rapidly displaced by Gigabit Ethernet (802.3z ratified 1998)
notes: Prediction (OBS-033) correct for near-term; FDDI lasted through mid-1990s as predicted but was largely obsolete by 2000.

metric_value: Competitive lead eroded quickly: Cisco and Bay Networks closed gap by 1997; 3Com acquired by HP in 2010 after struggles in enterprise networking
notes: Prediction (OBS-034) correct: pressure intensified. Cisco dominated enterprise switching by 1998. 3Com exited enterprise switching market by 2001.

metric_name: ATM as desktop/workgroup successor — actual outcome
metric_value: ATM did NOT become the desktop/workgroup successor; Gigabit Ethernet (1998) and 10GbE (2002) won. ATM limited to WAN/carrier backbones.
notes: Prediction (OBS-035) wrong: ATM lost workgroup battle to Ethernet. 3Com's HPSN vision of ATM desktop transition never materialized.
